attributes.name: Hidden PowerShell Window with Web Download Cradle
attributes.description: Detects PowerShell execution with hidden window style combined with web download cmdlets (Invoke-WebRequest, iwr, Net.WebClient). UNK_RobotDreams uses this pattern to stage the Rust backdoor binary via Azure Front Door CDN. Any attacker using hidden PowerShell with download cradles will trigger this detection.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.args : ("-w", "-WindowStyle") and process.args : ("hidden", "Hidden", "h") and process.args : ("*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*iwr*", "*Invoke-RestMethod*", "*Net.WebClient*", "*DownloadFile*", "*DownloadString*", "*Start-BitsTransfer*")
author: F0RT1KA sectest-builder
false_positives: Legitimate software updaters using hidden PowerShell with web requests

attributes.name: PowerShell ExecutionPolicy Bypass with File Download
attributes.description: Detects PowerShell execution combining ExecutionPolicy Bypass with file download cmdlets and output file parameters. UNK_RobotDreams uses this to ensure the download cradle runs regardless of endpoint policy. This is a common technique across multiple threat actors.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and process.args : ("*-ExecutionPolicy*", "*-ep*") and process.args : ("*Bypass*", "*bypass*") and process.args : ("*Invoke-WebRequest*", "*iwr*", "*-OutFile*", "*-outf*", "*DownloadFile*")
author: F0RT1KA sectest-builder

attributes.name: Non-Browser Process Connecting to Azure Front Door CDN
attributes.description: Detects non-browser processes making network connections to Azure Front Door (azurefd.net) or Azure CDN (azureedge.net) domains. UNK_RobotDreams uses Azure Front Door for domain fronting C2 communication, making malicious traffic appear as legitimate CDN requests. This detection flags suspicious processes accessing these CDN domains.
attributes.query: network where destination.domain : ("*.azurefd.net", "*.azureedge.net", "*.trafficmanager.net") and not process.name : ("msedge.exe", "chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "iexplore.exe", "Teams.exe", "OneDrive.exe", "outlook.exe", "svchost.exe", "MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe", "MsSense.exe", "MsMpEng.exe")

technique.name: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location

author: F0RT1KA sectest-builder

attributes.name: PDF File Creation with Co-located Executable in User Directory
attributes.description: Detects creation of PDF files alongside executable files in user-writable directories. This pattern is characteristic of spearphishing lure delivery where a PDF lure is placed alongside an executable loader, as used by UNK_RobotDreams with Gulf Security Alert PDFs and AdobeAcrobatUpdate.exe loaders.
attributes.query: sequence by host.name with maxspan=1m [file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension == "pdf" and file.path : "*\\Users\\*" and not file.path : ("*\\Program Files*", "*\\Windows\\*")] [file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension == "exe" and file.path : "*\\Users\\*" and not file.path : ("*\\Program Files*", "*\\Windows\\*")]
author: F0RT1KA sectest-builder
false_positives: PDF creation tools bundling helper executables

attributes.name: Adobe-Themed Executable Running from Non-Standard Path
attributes.description: Detects executables with Adobe-themed names (AdobeAcrobatUpdate, AdobeReader) running from directories outside of standard Adobe installation paths (Program Files). UNK_RobotDreams names its loader AdobeAcrobatUpdate.exe to masquerade as a legitimate Adobe update.
attributes.query: process where event.type == "start" and process.name : ("AdobeAcrobatUpdate.exe", "AdobeReaderUpdate.exe", "AdobeHelper.exe", "AdobeUpdate.exe") and not process.executable : ("C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*")

technique.name: Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location

author: F0RT1KA sectest-builder
false_positives: Very unlikely - legitimate Adobe updaters execute from Program Files
